decisions.decision: Only upgrade surfaces that have a FULL-TEXT owned source in .oracle/written-content/ (Access: free). Book entries (Access: book) are 1-2 sentence summaries and are NOT oracle-gateable -- leave those surfaces no-oracle.
decisions.rationale: A tier upgrade to 'owned; oracle-verified' is only honest if the surface can actually be gated against real source text.

decisions.decision: Re-gate every upgraded surface via the orchestrator-run oracle-reviewer; the executor authors/adjusts BLIND (never reads .oracle/).

context_notes: Phase 17 is done and committed (tree clean; only the ephemeral workflow._auto_chain_active flag was toggled and cleared). This follow-up RE-OPENS closed Phase-16 shipped content (three-laws-and-test-selection.md, test-structure-and-assertions.md STR slice) -- it is a legitimate owned-source upgrade enabled by the maintainer provisioning Beck's essays. The pattern to follow is exactly the Phase-17 clean-room re-gate loop: edit attribution/tier -> re-gate against the correct .oracle source -> converge-to-clean. principle-backing.md already carries a note that Beck's selection/structure rows 'remain no-oracle for now, pending a follow-up upgrade against his now-provisioned essays (Canon TDD, First One Then Many)'. Watch the instrument: check-red-references asserts content tokens (e.g. ADV-0) -- do not remove load-bearing tokens; run `node .claude/skills/lz-red-workspace/tools/check-red-references.mjs` + `npm --prefix .claude/skills/lz-red-workspace run typecheck` + `node .claude/skills/lz-refactor-workspace/tools/check-hygiene.mjs` after edits (all must stay exit 0). Use rg not grep; ASCII-only; commit identity = public gmail. Also-open follow-ups (NOT this task): #1 broader internal-ID/DST-04 3-skill polish; #3 FUT-METZ-REFACTOR lz-refactor Metz layer; and Phase 18 (coach procedure + lz-tpp seam).
